---
title: "13_TabPFN"
author: 'Andrea Polazzi'
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output: html_document
---

# Setup
```{r setup}
library(reticulate, quietly = T)
use_virtualenv("~/Desktop/CBM/project/analysis/.venv-tabpfn", required = TRUE)
py_config()
api_token <- Sys.getenv('TABPFN-TOKEN')
library(readxl, quietly = T)
library(tidyverse, quietly = T)
library(pROC, quietly = T)
library(caret, quietly = T)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
	echo = FALSE,
	message = FALSE,
	warning = FALSE
)
source('../scripts/clean_columns.R')

cache_file <- "../outputs/tabpfn_cv_results.rds"
run_tabpfn <- !file.exists(cache_file)

data <- read_xlsx('../data/raw/PCAWG_variables_PCA_woK.xlsx')
clean_data <- clean_pcawg_data(data)
model_data <- clean_data %>%
  select(-c(1, 2)) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.)) %>%
  mutate(
    alt_status = factor(
      alt_status,
      levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
    )
  )

summary(model_data)
```

# Data preparation for TabPFN

```{r split}
set.seed(21)
train_index <- sample(seq_len(nrow(model_data)), size = 0.7 * nrow(model_data))
train <- model_data[train_index, ]
test  <- model_data[-train_index, ]

# convert in 0/1 encoding, to be sure
y_train_r <- as.integer(train$alt_status == "ALT-high")
y_test_r  <- as.integer(test$alt_status  == "ALT-high")

x_train_r <- train %>% select(-alt_status)
x_test_r  <- test  %>% select(-alt_status)

x_all_r <- model_data %>% select(-alt_status)
y_all_r  <- as.integer(model_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
```

```{python tabpfn}
import numpy as np
import tabpfn_client
from tabpfn_client import TabPFNClassifier

tabpfn_client.set_access_token(r.api_token)

X_train = r.x_train_r.to_numpy().astype(float)
X_test  = r.x_test_r.to_numpy().astype(float)
y_train = np.array(r.y_train_r, dtype=int)

clf = TabPFNClassifier()
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)

tabpfn_probs = clf.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]
```

# Performance evaluation

We evaluate the performance of the TabPFN classifier with the same metrics used for the other models.

```{r evaluate, echo=FALSE}
tabpfn_prob <- py$tabpfn_probs
y_test <- y_test_r

roc_tabpfn <- roc(
  response = factor(y_test, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")),
  predictor = tabpfn_prob,
  levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"),
  direction = "<"
)

plot(roc_tabpfn, main = "ROC curve - TabPFN (all features)")
auc(roc_tabpfn)
```

These **preliminary results** obtained on a first split of the data are already **promising**: the model achieved an **AUC = 0.9942**

```{r confusion-matrix}
pred_class <- factor(
  ifelse(tabpfn_prob >= 0.5, "ALT-high", "ALT-low"),
  levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
)
true_class <- factor(
  ifelse(y_test == 1, "ALT-high", "ALT-low"),
  levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
)

confusionMatrix(pred_class, true_class, positive = "ALT-high")
```

The confusion matrix, although being calculated on a single split, shows a good behavior, concordant with previous results: a **good, significant accuracy (0.988)**, **perfect specificity (1.000)** and **high sensitivity (0.800)**, that together yield a **good balanced accuracy (0.900)**.
 
# Stratified CV

To have a fair comparison between Log. Reg. and RF models, we have used the same **5-fold stratified CV with 5 repeats**.

Here are reported the results and performance evaluation metrics:

```{r cv-folds}
set.seed(21)

cv_data <- model_data
k <- 5
n_repeats <- 5

cv_splits <- list()
split_id <- 1

for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
  fold_id <- integer(nrow(cv_data))
  
  idx_low <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-low")
  idx_high <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
  
  fold_id[idx_low] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_low)))
  fold_id[idx_high] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_high)))
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_idx <- which(fold_id != fold)
    test_idx <- which(fold_id == fold)
    
    cv_splits[[split_id]] <- list(
      train = train_idx,
      test = test_idx,
      rep = r,
      fold = fold
    )
    
    split_id <- split_id + 1
  }
}
```

```{python tabpfn-cv, eval=run_tabpfn}
import numpy as np
from tabpfn_client import TabPFNClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score

X_all = r.x_all_r.to_numpy().astype(float)
y_all = np.array(r.y_all_r, dtype=int)

cv_splits = r.cv_splits

auc_scores = []

per_fold_metrics = []

for split in cv_splits:
    train_idx = np.array(split["train"], dtype=int) - 1
    test_idx  = np.array(split["test"],  dtype=int) - 1

    clf = TabPFNClassifier()
    clf.fit(X_all[train_idx], y_all[train_idx])

    probs     = clf.predict_proba(X_all[test_idx])[:, 1]
    preds     = (probs >= 0.5).astype(int)
    true      = y_all[test_idx]

    auc  = roc_auc_score(true, probs)
    tp   = int(np.sum((preds == 1) & (true == 1)))
    fp   = int(np.sum((preds == 1) & (true == 0)))
    tn   = int(np.sum((preds == 0) & (true == 0)))
    fn   = int(np.sum((preds == 0) & (true == 1)))

    # sensitivity e specificity: NA-safe
    sens = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else float("nan")
    spec = tn / (tn + fp) if (tn + fp) > 0 else float("nan")

    per_fold_metrics.append({
        "auc": auc, "tp": tp, "fp": fp, "tn": tn, "fn": fn,
        "sensitivity": sens, "specificity": spec
    })
```

```{r load-or-save-tabpfn}
if (file.exists(cache_file)) {
  results <- readRDS(cache_file)
  message("Loaded cached TabPFN results")
} else {
  results <- list(
    per_fold_metrics = py$per_fold_metrics %>%
      bind_rows() %>%
      mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric)),
    seed = 21,
    k = k,
    n_repeats = n_repeats
  )
  saveRDS(results, cache_file)
  message("Computed TabPFN results and saved")
}

metrics_df <- results$per_fold_metrics
```

```{r cv-results}
cat('=== Previous results for reference ===\nLogistic Regression: ~0.94 ± 0.083\nRandom Forest:       ~0.98 ± 0.012')

# Confusion matrix aggregata (metodo 1)
agg_tp <- sum(metrics_df$tp)
agg_fp <- sum(metrics_df$fp)
agg_tn <- sum(metrics_df$tn)
agg_fn <- sum(metrics_df$fn)

cat("=== Aggregated confusion matrix (sum over all CV folds) ===\n")
cm_agg <- matrix(c(agg_tn, agg_fn,
                   agg_fp, agg_tp),
                 nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE,
                 dimnames = list(
                   Predicted = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"),
                   Reference = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
                 ))
print(cm_agg)

cat("Aggregated sensitivity:", round(agg_tp / (agg_tp + agg_fn), 4), "\nAggregated specificity:", round(agg_tn / (agg_tn + agg_fp), 4), "\nAggregated balanced accuracy:", round(0.5 * (agg_tp/(agg_tp+agg_fn) + agg_tn/(agg_tn+agg_fp)), 4))

# Metriche per-fold con media ± SD (metodo 2)
cat("=== Per-fold metrics (mean ± SD across 25 folds) ===\n")
metrics_df %>%
  summarise(
    across(c(auc, sensitivity, specificity),
           list(mean = ~round(mean(.x, na.rm=TRUE), 4),
                sd   = ~round(sd(.x,   na.rm=TRUE), 4)))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to  = c("metric", ".value"),
               names_sep = "_(?=[^_]+$)") %>%
  mutate(summary = paste0(mean, " ± ", sd)) %>%
  select(metric, summary) %>%
  print()
```

# Interpretability

```{r export-data, eval=FALSE}
dir.create("../outputs/shap_input", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

readr::write_csv(as.data.frame(x_train_r), "../outputs/shap_input/X_train.csv")
n_shap <- min(20, nrow(x_test_r))
readr::write_csv(as.data.frame(x_test_r[1:n_shap, , drop = FALSE]),
                 "../outputs/shap_input/X_test_small.csv")    # we use a smaller test set otherwise it takes too much time
readr::write_csv(data.frame(y_train = y_train_r), "../outputs/shap_input/y_train.csv")
```

```{r run-python-script, eval=FALSE}
cmd <- paste(
  "source ~/Desktop/CBM/project/analysis/.venv-tabpfn/bin/activate &&",
  "python ~/Desktop/CBM/project/scripts/run_tabpfn_shap.py"
)

Sys.setenv(TABPFN_TOKEN = api_token)

system(cmd, intern = FALSE, ignore.stdout = FALSE, ignore.stderr = FALSE)
```

![](../outputs/tabpfn_shap1.png)
![](../outputs/tabpfn_shap2.png)

![](../outputs/tabpfn_shap3.png)


TODO: feature ablation, try inference of TERT, try extensions (ensembles, interpretability, RF)
